record.text: Another fun thing is that if a state helps pay for someone to live in a nursing home through Medicaid, when that person dies, the state can claim compensation from the estate to recover what they spent. Real fun when the estate has $3k (like my dad) and they’re harassing you for $350k. 😐

value.text: Oh yeah that is something most people don't know about TANF. States are allowed to demand that families sign over some/all their child support payments to compensate them for the cash assistance they provide the family. States have different passthrough rates.

UBI is about creating a social structure so that your life and the lives of your loved ones won't be destroyed if misfortune should befall you.

THIS is the framing that needs to be used.

images.alt:
‘Non-stop’ bombing of eastern Rafah ongoing

There has been an escalation of air raids and artillery bombardment in the eastern part of Rafah.

We’re talking nonstop bombing of residential houses. The vast majority of residents there [eastern Rafah] have started to flee, where the Israeli military is trying to mobilise more troops.

Eyewitnesses that we have been in contact with say they have heard movement of Israeli military tanks across the Gaza separation fence with the Israel.

That’s absolutely terrifying … and contradictory to the general atmosphere of positivity around the negotiations on the ground.

Continue on with ‘complete defeat’ of Hamas: Israeli minister
Israel’s far-right national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, has responded to Hamas’s agreement to a truce proposal. He says the group is playing “games” that have only “one answer, an immediate order to occupy Rafah!”

“Increasing military pressure, and continuing the complete defeat of Hamas, until its complete defeat,” he added in a social media post.

Ben-Gvir has been among the members of the Israeli government urging Netanyahu to continue fighting in Gaza until Hamas is defeated.

value.text: Bibi’s cabinet seem to be encouraging him to decline the ceasefire agreement and to just invade Rafah. Hold on to your hats folks. I don’t think this is yet over.

images.alt:
Moss, 60, had been in Kensington visiting a friend, he said, when NSF Squad 2 grabbed him. Roher said he saw Moss make two hand-to-hand transactions. Police found no drugs on Moss, but he was still charged with dealing based on Roher’s report.

Moss said he had been employed as a janitor — but lost his job after missing work while in jail. Out on bail, he found another job. But, after being locked up on a warrant for missing a court date, he lost that job, too.

“I secured gainful employment,” he said, “and it was just snatched away from me.”

Prosecutors twice sent Roher subpoenas to come to the DA’s office to discuss the case. Both times, Roher “failed to appear with no explanation,” according to a court filing.

At least 70 cases Roher has worked on have been dropped, according to Mellon.

Roher, 40 — who has been the subject of 24 civilian complaints, among the most in the department, a 2020 WHYY article found — didn’t respond to messages requesting comment.

Moss has struggled to get back on his feet after a previous prison term. He said the experience only hardened his belief that the system is rigged.

The day his case was dropped, he said, he was afraid even to go to court — fearful of any further interaction with police.

“I could become a target. Wherever I go, I might be patted down, or they might find something in my pocket I know I never put in my pocket,” he said. “We already know they’re crooked. Now, who’s to say they won’t be crooked again?”

record.text: the philly police department should institute a policy where, if your name ends up in the newspaper, then you are off the street for a year. how does roher still have a fucking job?

images.alt: Chart showing the gender gap in Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Songs of All Time list. It's split into three parts for 2003, 2012, and 2020. Each part has two bars—one for albums by women artists and one for albums by men artists. In 2003, men have 410 albums, while women have only 38. Though the gap narrows slightly by 2020, men still have 358 albums compared to 93 for women.

images.alt:
The ad called out both Capcom's Breath of Fire and Enix's Brain Lord, telling players that once they've mastered both you could be "ready for a role-playing game where the sun doesn't shing and the monsters grow big, really big!"

The ad boasted details about the game's features such as "55 different monsters from the Underworld", "50 different items that will help you in your quest", and "five threacherous levels" to fight through as you try to find your way back to the surface. 

The box is also shown with its illustration of the sorceress that is after your head front and center.

record.text: The ad for Brandish promised adventure and for eager explorers, it delivered another fascinating chapter from Nihon Falcom's creative repertoire. A remake was even released for the PSP in 2009, introducing a new generation to Ares' (or Varik as he was called in North America's release) adventures!

“No, they’re inside.”
“Your wife?”
“Not here.”
“… am I the adult right now?”
“Yeah, you’ll be fine.”

images.alt:
Joy does not arrive with a fanfare

on a red carpet strewn

with the flowers of a perfect life

joy sneaks in

as you pour a cup of coffee

watching the sun

hit your favourite tree

just right

and you usher joy away

because you are not ready for her

your house is not as it should be

for such a distinguished guest

but joy, you see

cares nothing for your messy home,

or your bank balance

or your waistline

joy is supposed to slither through

the cracks of your imperfect life

that’s how joy works

you cannot truly invite her

you can only be ready

when she appears

and hug her with meaning

because in this very moment

joy chose you.

record.text:
joy is supposed to slither through

the cracks of your imperfect life

that’s how joy works - Joy Chose you by Donna Ashworth
#Poetry #LiteratureSky 💙📚👀

images.alt:
Joy does not arrive with a fanfare

on a red carpet strewn

with the flowers of a perfect life

joy sneaks in

as you pour a cup of coffee

watching the sun

hit your favourite tree

just right

and you usher joy away

because you are not ready for her

your house is not as it should be

for such a distinguished guest

but joy, you see

cares nothing for your messy home,

or your bank balance

or your waistline

joy is supposed to slither through

the cracks of your imperfect life

that’s how joy works

you cannot truly invite her

you can only be ready

when she appears

and hug her with meaning

because in this very moment

joy chose you.

images.alt:
of the Christian Democratic Union of Germany, in a commentary for the Neue Osnabrücker Zeitung.Kiesewetter proposed that a "coalition of the willing" could extend air defense up to 70 to 100 kilometers into western Ukraine. This would ease the burden on Ukraine’s Armed Forces, enabling them to focus on air defense further east. He highlighted that some countries, like Lithuania, are considering sending field hospitals, demining engineers, or logistics and repair troops to Ukraine, actions he deemed "perfectly permissible" under international law and sensible from a security policy perspective.
"We should not rule anything out, and within the framework of the coalition of the willing, we should find out who can do it," Kiesewetter emphasized.
He also criticized the German government, stating that most of Germany’s aid to Ukraine is given as cash payments to Ukrainians living in Germany, and only 30% of the publicly declared aid actually reaches Ukraine.

images.alt: Weather map of Finland for end of May in 2024, showing temperatures of 77°F to 82°F with thunderstorms in southern Finland.

record.text:
Things are hot in Finland, and guess who has a broken fridge. 

Noticed it wasn't working properly last week, and it broke down during the weekend. Took a few days to get hold of the right landlord, but a new fridge should be here by Friday.

Until then, no ice cubes or ice-cream for me 😒
